topic: New flagship topic: proximal difference-in-differences bridge frontier with negative controls. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchors are Miao-Geng-Tchetgen proximal causal inference, Tchetgen-Tchetgen negative-control bridge work, standard two-period and staggered DiD identification, and recent papers on difference-in-differences with negative controls. Our theorem is not those because the focal object is a finite pretrend-proxy bridge operator that sharply separates three regimes: ordinary parallel-trends DiD identifies ATT, proximal negative-control DiD identifies ATT even when parallel trends fails, and neither identifies ATT. Require a concrete nonroutine object: an explicit 2-group x 3-period x binary-proxy witness with observed negative-control outcome and exposure, a bridge-rank condition computed from pre-period moments, and a non-equivalence theorem showing the proximal bridge and parallel-trends restrictions are neither nested nor equivalent. If the proposal reduces to generic proximal completeness, ordinary DiD placebo tests, support coverage, or a definition-unfold rank condition without a worked witness and non-equivalence class, pivot or stop early.

open_problems.open_problem: Construct a full 2-group x 3-period x binary-proxy observed/latent law whose observed preperiod moments compute a bridge-rank condition and whose postperiod ATT falls into the parallel-trends-only, proximal-only, and neither-identifies regimes.

source_refs.claim: Two independent proxy variables satisfying rank/completeness conditions can identify causal effects with unmeasured confounding, but the paper does not give a finite DiD pretrend bridge frontier.
source_refs.author: Wang Miao, Zhi Geng, Eric J. Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: Double negative-control bridge functions identify causal effects from a negative-control outcome and exposure, but not in a 2-group x 3-period DiD regime-separation witness.
source_refs.author: Wang Miao, Xu Shi, Yilin Li, Eric J. Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: NC-DiD uses multiple negative-control outcomes to test and calibrate parallel-trends violations, but it is NCO-only and does not solve an NCO plus NCE bridge-rank problem.
source_refs.author: Dazheng Zhang, Bingyu Zhang, Huiyuan Wang, Yiwen Lu, Charles J. Wolock, Wenjie Hu, Linbo Wang, George Hripcsak, Yong Chen

source_refs.excerpt: moment rows rather than a full finite observed/latent law computing every object from Section 6 primitives
does not give a full law or latent completion satisfying the causal assumptions

open_problems.why_unsolved: The proximal papers provide bridge/rank identification conditions and the NC-DiD paper provides negative-control calibration for parallel-trends violations, but neither source supplies the finite observed/latent panel law that computes bridge rank, DiD, ATT, and negative-control exclusions from raw cells. The v1 reviewer rejected the same topic precisely because its evidence was moment rows rather than a full finite law and latent completion.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to invoke the generic proximal rank theorem fails because the v1 reviewer notes that moment rows do not provide a full finite observed/latent law or latent completion satisfying the causal assumptions.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A positive-cell finite probability table over group, period, treatment, outcome, negative-control outcome, negative-control exposure, and latent type, with explicit bridge matrix, rank, DiD contrast, ATT, and bridge-corrected ATT calculations for all three regimes.

open_problems.open_problem: Prove finite non-nesting of ordinary parallel trends, generalized DiD equi-confounding, and proximal negative-control bridge restrictions by giving observed-data laws where each restriction identifies ATT without the others and a law where all fail.

source_refs.claim: Frames DiD as a negative-outcome-control method and explains that bias-equivalence restrictions are scale-dependent, but it does not compare DiD with double-proxy bridge-rank restrictions.
source_refs.author: Tamar Sofer, David B. Richardson, Elena Colicino, Joel Schwartz, Eric J. Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: Generalized DiD uses alternative equi-confounding restrictions, but does not provide a proximal double-negative-control bridge-rank separation theorem.
source_refs.author: David B. Richardson, Ting Ye, Eric J. Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: Universal DiD replaces parallel trends with odds-ratio equi-confounding for broader outcome types, but remains a DiD/equi-confounding framework rather than a double-proxy bridge frontier.
source_refs.author: Eric J. Tchetgen Tchetgen, Chan Park, David B. Richardson

source_refs.excerpt: separating parallel-trends-only, proximal-only, and nonidentified regimes

open_problems.why_unsolved: The DiD/negative-control literature separates additive parallel trends, generalized or universal equi-confounding, and double-negative-control bridge equations as different identifying devices, but the sources do not give a shared finite signature that proves their strict non-inclusions. The prior proposal named parallel-trends-only, proximal-only, and nonidentified regimes, yet the reviewer found the branch witnesses too incomplete to establish a robust non-equivalence theorem.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to compare the restrictions by prose fails because the published papers formulate separate DiD and bridge assumptions while the v1 trail only named regimes without explicit finite laws and latent completions.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite catalogue of rational-valued observed/latent laws on common support certifying parallel-trends-only, proximal-bridge-only, generalized-DiD-only if included, both, and neither regimes.

open_problems.open_problem: Separate a preperiod proxy bridge-rank diagnostic from ordinary pretrend, placebo, lead-coefficient, and sensitivity diagnostics by proving finite cases where those diagnostics disagree about ATT identification.

source_refs.claim: Develops robust inference and sensitivity analysis for violations of parallel trends using pretrend information, but not negative-control bridge-rank identification.
source_refs.author: Ashesh Rambachan, Jonathan Roth

source_refs.claim: Identifies ATT parameters with multiple periods and treatment timing under conditional parallel trends, but not with negative-control proxy bridge rank.
source_refs.author: Brantly Callaway, Pedro H. C. SantAnna

source_refs.claim: Shows event-study/TWFE complications under heterogeneous treatment effects, but does not address negative-control proximal bridges.
source_refs.author: Liyang Sun, Sarah Abraham

source_refs.excerpt: sensitivity threshold alone is not a named-estimator non-equivalence frontier
explicit named-estimator score matrices, observable thresholds, and small witness laws

open_problems.why_unsolved: Modern DiD work explains sensitivity, staggered/event-study identification, and pretrend diagnostics under parallel-trends-style restrictions, while the prior panel artifacts warn that a robustness threshold is not itself a named-estimator non-equivalence frontier. The missing obstacle is an observable negative-control moment operator that can be computed in the preperiod and shown to diverge logically from familiar pretrend or placebo diagnostics.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to treat bridge rank as another pretrend test fails because Rambachan-Roth-style sensitivity and prior panel reviews concern robustness diagnostics, not finite identification-frontier laws where the diagnostics provably disagree.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An iff theorem mapping preperiod negative-control moments to a finite bridge operator, plus witness laws where pretrend or lead checks pass but bridge identification fails and where bridge rank succeeds despite nonparallel untreated trends.

literature_map:
1. Miao-Geng-Tchetgen 2018 is the core proxy-rank ancestor: two independent proxies can identify causal effects under unmeasured confounding.
2. Miao-Shi-Li-Tchetgen 2024 gives the double-negative-control bridge-function formulation, but not in a DiD panel frontier.
3. Cui-Pu-Shi-Miao-Tchetgen 2024 gives semiparametric proximal inference around bridge equations, again not a finite DiD/non-nesting witness.
4. Sofer-Richardson-Colicino-Schwartz-Tchetgen 2016 explicitly links DiD to negative outcome controls and scale-sensitive bias-equivalence assumptions.
5. Richardson-Ye-Tchetgen 2023 generalizes DiD by changing equi-confounding assumptions, not by adding a double-proxy bridge operator.
6. Tchetgen-Park-Richardson 2024 universal DiD further relaxes additive parallel trends for nonlinear outcomes via odds-ratio equi-confounding.
7. Zhang-Zhang-Wang et al. 2025 is the closest negative-control DiD anchor: multiple NCOs detect/calibrate bias from parallel-trends violations.
8. That 2025 NC-DiD paper appears NCO-only and calibration-oriented, not a proximal NCO+NCE rank theorem.
9. Imbens-Kallus-Mao 2021 shows bridge functions can be natural in panel data/factor models, but not as negative-control bridge rank in finite DiD.
10. Callaway-SantAnna 2021, Sun-Abraham 2021, and Goodman-Bacon 2021 define the modern staggered/event-study DiD comparison class.
11. Rambachan-Roth 2023 is the pretrend/sensitivity anchor; it studies robust inference around parallel-trends violations, not proxy bridge identification.
12. Net scan result: the finite 2-group x 3-period x binary-proxy bridge witness and non-equivalence theorem remains a real frontier, with Zhang et al. 2025 and generalized/universal DiD as the main comparators.
prior_proposal_map:
1. Direct prior: eid_proximal_did_bridge_frontier_v1 is the main adjacent failed run; reviewer rejected moment-row witnesses and definition-unfold bridge fibers.
2. Current v2 pre-dispatcher-fix artifacts refactor v1 into useful subproblems: full law, primitives, non-nesting, fallback interval, diagnostic disagreement.
3. eid_proximal_phase_v1 supplies the broader proximal bridge-null / rank-deficient fallback thread and repeatedly asks for finite endpoint certificates.
4. eid_binary_did_scale_frontier_v1 is adjacent on DiD non-equivalence: additive PT versus OREC scale restrictions need open-cell witness families.
5. panel_honest_did_phase_v1 and pid_tv_pretrend_atet_v1 are adjacent DiD sensitivity/pretrend artifacts but not proxy-bridge theorems.
6. q_did_drdid_ipwdid_frontier_v1 is useful as a named-estimator-frontier warning: require observable thresholds and small witness laws.
7. Strongest carry-forward gate: do not accept generic proximal completeness, placebo/pretrend diagnostics, or bridge-rank definitions without raw finite laws and non-equivalence classes.
